text: Define a project-local autonomy policy file under .bowman/runtime
description: Decide how Aimparency should represent durable local autonomy policy: operator constraints, allowed tools, compaction/commit rules, recovery preferences, and future constitution-like guidance. This should likely live under .bowman/runtime or a closely related project-local file.

reflections.context: Define a project-local autonomy policy file so future autonomy behavior is anchored in an inspectable runtime document instead of only prompts and code paths.
reflections.outcome: Added .bowman/runtime/autonomy-policy.json defaults and backend get/update endpoints. The policy now has an explicit place for autonomy mode, preferred agent type, lease duration, reconnect behavior, and commit-before-compact rules.
reflections.effectiveness: This was effective because it established a concrete file boundary without forcing a UI redesign first. The backend can now own policy reads/writes and the UI can be layered on later.
reflections.lesson: For autonomy features, define the durable file contract early. It reduces future prompt sprawl and makes policy decisions inspectable and testable.
reflections.pattern: This mirrors the broader Aimparency architecture: once a concept has a file-backed model and a backend API, the frontend and worker logic become easier to align around it.

status.comment: Defined and scaffolded .bowman/runtime/autonomy-policy.json with backend read/write endpoints. The file now provides an explicit local policy boundary for autonomy mode, lease defaults, reconnect behavior, and commit-before-compact rules.
